podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: Is the film camera actually making a comeback? #sharktank #entrepreneur #business
podcasts.transcript: A 23-year-old founder walks into the tank with a camera that brought film photography back from the dead. And today, the Sharks are about to go to war over her. >> I'm seeking a $300,000 investment for 10% equity in the company. I invested all of my savings, [music] $50,000, into developing a film camera of my own. So, in August of 2021, I launched the Reload reusable film camera to market. Today, we've sold over 40,000 units and we're stocked [music] in major Australian retailers, The Iconic and Universal Store. >> Well done. Wow. >> What's the price of the individual unit? >> So, $99 per camera and lifetime revenue we've done 3.6 million. 3.6 million? Over what period of time? >> Just over a year and a half. And what were your sales last year? >> Last year, we did 1.89 million. Did you make any money on that? >> So, we sit at a [music] 10% net profit. >> Sharks aren't here for nostalgia. They want answers. Why are you here? Uh Why do you need this money? Why you only got 10% margins? Oh, that's a hard question. You know, you had a little trouble with the answering the finance question. You're absolutely killing it, but for now, unfortunately, [music] I'm out. >> I don't think you need investment. >> Three Sharks down. And then, Jane and I will give you 300,000, but we want a little more equity. We'll do it for 20% straight equity. >> What if you met in the middle and do 15? 18. Can we meet at 17 and a half? DONE. GREAT DECISION. OH.
